criteria_scores.evidence: Delivery costs are displayed clearly on every product card and in the basket summary, with a prominent free shipping threshold banner shown throughout the journey.
Auto-delivery options are available but subscription management is buried in account settings rather than surfaced at point of purchase.
The basket total is shown but VAT breakdown and savings are not consistently surfaced, creating uncertainty before checkout.
Product images and names appear clearly in the cart with variant details shown.
Mobile basket layout is clean and thumb-friendly with a persistent sticky checkout CTA that remains visible while scrolling.
Only PayPal is offered as an express checkout option; Apple Pay and Google Pay are absent despite being the market standard. This creates significant friction for mobile users.
Shipping costs are shown at basket stage but require user to enter postcode before seeing final delivery price.

No subscription or auto-delivery option exists on the platform. Repeat purchase journeys require manual re-ordering, putting the site at a significant disadvantage versus competitors offering autoship.
The basket clearly shows itemised costs, total savings from promotions, and the running total updates in real time as quantities are adjusted without a page reload.
Product names are shown but images are small on mobile and variant details such as weight or flavour are truncated.
Mobile layout is functional and well-spaced with reasonable tap targets, though the checkout button is not sticky on long baskets.
No express checkout options are present. Users must complete full guest or account registration before paying, adding significant friction at the most critical conversion point.

annotations.text: Basket summary could be more prominent

criteria_scores.evidence: Free shipping eligibility is surfaced immediately in the basket with a clear progress bar showing how much more to spend. Paid shipping costs are shown upfront without requiring address entry.

Autoship is deeply integrated into the basket — every eligible product shows the autoship price reduction inline with a one-click toggle. Frequency selection is intuitive and the savings are quantified clearly.
The basket total section is clear and itemised, though the autoship and one-time totals are shown separately which can cause brief confusion when switching between modes.
Full product details including image, name, size, flavour, and quantity are shown in the cart. Users can edit variant selections without leaving the cart view.
Mobile cart is excellently optimised with a persistent sticky order summary and checkout button. Touch targets are generous and the layout collapses cleanly to a single-column view.
Despite strong UX elsewhere, express checkout options are limited to PayPal. Apple Pay is not supported which is a notable gap given the proportion of mobile traffic to pet retail sites.
